---
title: "Regresión logística"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r carga de datos}
load("listings_modificado.RData")
```

```{r librerias}

```

```{r separacion de datos train/test}
set.seed(123)

muestra_idx <- sample(1:nrow(listings), size = 10000)
muestra <- listings[muestra_idx, ]

n <- nrow(muestra)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = 0.7 * n)

train <- muestra[train_idx, ]
test <- muestra[-train_idx, ]
```

```{r variable catrgorica de precio}
cuts <- quantile(train$price, probs = c(0.33, 0.66), na.rm = TRUE)

train$price_cat <- cut(train$price,
                       breaks = c(-Inf, cuts[1], cuts[2], Inf),
                       labels = c("Economica", "Intermedia", "Cara"))

test$price_cat <- cut(test$price,
                      breaks = c(-Inf, cuts[1], cuts[2], Inf),
                      labels = c("Economica", "Intermedia", "Cara"))
```

```{r variables dicotómicas}
# TRAIN
train$econ <- ifelse(train$price_cat == "Economica", 1, 0)
train$inter <- ifelse(train$price_cat == "Intermedia", 1, 0)
train$cara <- ifelse(train$price_cat == "Cara", 1, 0)

# TEST
test$econ <- ifelse(test$price_cat == "Economica", 1, 0)
test$inter <- ifelse(test$price_cat == "Intermedia", 1, 0)
test$cara <- ifelse(test$price_cat == "Cara", 1, 0)
```

```{r parte 3}


library(caret)
library(car)
library(corrplot)


train$cara_factor <- factor(ifelse(train$cara == 1, "Si", "No"), levels = c("No", "Si"))
test$cara_factor <- factor(ifelse(test$cara == 1, "Si", "No"), levels = c("No", "Si"))


set.seed(123)

# Configurar Validación Cruzada de 10 pliegues
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", 
                     number = 10, 
                     savePredictions = "final", 
                     classProbs = TRUE)


modelo_logistico <- train(cara_factor ~ accommodates + bedrooms + bathrooms_num + number_of_reviews, 
                          data = train, 
                          method = "glm", 
                          family = "binomial", 
                          trControl = ctrl)

summary(modelo_logistico)


predicciones_prob <- predict(modelo_logistico, newdata = train, type = "prob")
predicciones_clase <- ifelse(predicciones_prob$Si >= 0.5, "Si", "No")
```


```{r parte 4}

# 1. Determinación de Multicolinealidad en las variables mediante VIF
cat("\n--- Análisis de Multicolinealidad (VIF) ---\n")
vif_valores <- vif(modelo_logistico$finalModel)
print(vif_valores)

# 2. Análisis de Correlación de las variables del modelo
vars_numericas <- train[, c("accommodates", "bedrooms", "bathrooms_num", "number_of_reviews")]
matriz_cor <- cor(vars_numericas, use = "complete.obs")

cat("\n--- Matriz de Correlación ---\n")
corrplot(matriz_cor, method = "color", type = "upper", 
         tl.col = "black", addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.8)

# 3. Especificar si el modelo se adapta bien a los datos 
mcfadden_r2 <- with(summary(modelo_logistico$finalModel), 1 - deviance/null.deviance)
cat("\n--- Bondad de Ajuste: Pseudo R-cuadrado (McFadden) ---\n")
print(paste("Valor de ajuste:", round(mcfadden_r2, 4)))
```
